  • .rst

oceanicospy documentation

oceanicospy documentation#

This is the documentation for oceanicospy, a Python package for handling most of the programming tasks related with analysis of in-situ data, reading observations from a set of differentes sensors, automatize the modelling worfkflow and plotting some common and practical figures.

Add your content using reStructuredText syntax. See the reStructuredText documentation for details.

Contents:

  • Installation
    • For users
      • Google Colab (recommended)
      • Local environment
    • Importing the library
    • Package structure
    • For developers
      • 1. Create a GitHub account
      • 1. Fork the repository
      • 1. Set up SSH authentication
      • 4. Clone your fork
      • 5. Install in editable mode
      • 6. Create a feature branch
      • 7. Make changes and commit
      • 8. Open a pull request
  • Analysis
    • Signal conditioning
      • Nyquist frequency
      • Aliasing
      • Windowing
      • Sliding windowing
      • Welch method
      • Smoothing
    • Spectral
      • WaveSpectralAnalyzer
    • Walkthrough Example - Spectral Analysis
    • Importing required modules and subpackages
    • Pressure data
    • Using the WaveSpectralAnalyzer class
      • Welch’s and FFT method
      • Wavelets transform
    • Temporal
      • WaveTemporalAnalyzer
    • Walkthrough Example - Temporal Analysis
    • Importing required modules and subpackages
    • Pressure data
    • Using the WaveTemporalAnalyzer class
      • Zero upcrossing method
    • Empirical mode decomposition (EMD) method
